mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) signal strengthening consumer demand and economic growth expectations, which increase inflation expectations and real yields, driving 10-year Treasury yields upward over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 344649M as of July 2014 reflects sustained consumer spending
regime_hint: post-crisis recovery with stable growth expectations
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and 20-day Treasury yield changes falls below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS surprises fail to precede yield increases in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) declining below 7.0% signals tightening labor market and wage pressure, which raises inflation expectations and compels the Fed to maintain higher real rates, pushing 10-year Treasury yields higher over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 7.6% as of July 2014, approaching historical tightening threshold
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market normalization
failure_condition: When UNRATE crosses below 7.0%, 10-year Treasury yields decline in the subsequent 5 trading days in > 50% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) held at near-zero levels (0.15%) combined with declining inflation expectations (CPIAUCSL growth slowing) creates a real rate floor that constrains downside yield movement, keeping 10-year Treasury yields stable to higher over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25% AND CPIAUCSL monthly growth < 0.2%
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.15% and CPIAUCSL at 211.49 as of June 2014
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary policy with disinflation risk
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields decline by > 15 bps in > 45% of 20-day periods when both trigger conditions are met, or joint predictive power (measured by interaction term coefficient) is not statistically significant at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above 0.3% month-over-month, signaling supply-side capacity expansion and productivity gains that support nominal growth and equity valuations, leading to higher 10-year Treasury yields over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.5966 (2017=100) as of June 2014, reflecting post-crisis recovery
regime_hint: supply-side recovery with capacity utilization normalization
failure_condition: 60-day forward Treasury yields decline when INDPRO growth exceeds 0.3% in > 50% of instances, or rolling 3-year Rank IC between INDPRO and yield changes remains below 0.06.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline below 550K units signals weakening housing demand and forward economic growth expectations, reducing inflation expectations and causing 10-year Treasury yields to fall over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 550K units as of June 2014, at critical threshold
regime_hint: demand-side slowdown with housing market deceleration
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise in > 55% of 20-day periods following HOUST drops below 500K, or Rank IC between HOUST changes and yield direction is below 0.05 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 150K per month combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE > 7.5%) signals labor market weakness that reduces growth expectations and inflation risk, pushing 10-year Treasury yields lower over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 134580K and UNRATE at 7.6% as of July 2014
regime_hint: labor market stagnation with weak job creation
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise in > 50% of 60-day periods when both conditions are met, or the interaction coefficient between PAYEMS and UNRATE is not statistically significant at p < 0.10 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates above 2.5% annualized rate combined with rising retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust aggregate demand that increases nominal growth and inflation expectations, driving 10-year Treasury yields higher over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth > 2.5% AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.4%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11599.4B (2017$) and RSAFS at 344649M as of Q2 2014
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion with strong consumption
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields decline in > 50% of 20-day periods when both trigger conditions are satisfied, or joint interaction term fails to achieve statistical significance (p > 0.10) in rolling 2-year regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 0.25% month-over-month while federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains at 0.15% creates negative real rates that signal monetary accommodation ending, prompting market expectations of future rate increases and higher 10-year Treasury yields over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 211.49 and FEDFUNDS at 0.15% as of June-July 2014
regime_hint: monetary policy normalization with inflation re-emergence
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields decline in > 55% of 5-day periods when both conditions are met, or the interaction coefficient between inflation and policy rate spread is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) over rolling 1-year windows.
